name: Random Forest Regression
description: Random Forest Regression builds an ensemble of decision trees to predict continuous numeric outcomes using both continuous and categorical features.
input: This takes in a DataFrame and performs Random Forest Regression
output: It generates the Random Forest Regression Model and passes it to the next Predict and ModelSave Nodes. The input DataFrame is also passed along to the next nodes.

fields.name: featuresCol
fields.title: Features Column
fields.description: Vector column containing all input features used to train the regression model.

fields.name: labelCol
fields.title: Label Column
fields.description: Target column containing the continuous numeric value the model is trained to predict.

fields.name: predictionCol
fields.title: Prediction Column
fields.description: Output column where predicted numeric values are written after model scoring.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Controls the proportion of data used for training versus validation.

fields.name: featureSubsetStrategy
fields.title: Feature Subset Strategy
fields.description: Defines how many features are randomly considered at each split to reduce correlation between trees.

fields.name: impurity
fields.title: Impurity
fields.description: Metric used to evaluate the quality of splits when predicting continuous values.

fields.name: maxBins
fields.title: Max Bins
fields.description: Maximum number of bins used to discretize feature values for split evaluation.

fields.name: maxDepth
fields.title: Max Depth
fields.description: Limits how deep each decision tree can grow, controlling model complexity.

fields.name: minInfoGain
fields.title: Min Information Gain
fields.description: Minimum improvement required before a split is accepted.

fields.name: minInstancesPerNode
fields.title: Min Instances Per Node
fields.description: Minimum number of records required in each leaf node to ensure stable predictions.

fields.name: numTrees
fields.title: Num Trees
fields.description: Number of decision trees trained in the ensemble.

fields.name: subsamplingRate
fields.title: Subsampling Rate
fields.description: Fraction of the training data sampled for each tree.

fields.name: seed
fields.title: Seed
fields.description: Seed value used to ensure reproducible model training.

fields.name: cacheNodeIds
fields.title: Cache Node Ids
fields.description: Caches intermediate tree computations to improve performance for deep forests.

fields.name: checkpointInterval
fields.title: Checkpoint Interval
fields.description: Frequency at which model training state is checkpointed to manage memory usage.

fields.name: maxMemoryInMB
fields.title: Max memory
fields.description: Maximum memory allocated for histogram-based computations.

fields.name: minWeightFractionPerNode
fields.title: Min weight fraction per node
fields.description: Ensures each split retains a minimum proportion of weighted data.

fields.name: bootstrap
fields.title: Bootstrap
fields.description: Controls whether bootstrap sampling is used when training trees.

fields.name: weightCol
fields.title: Weight Column
fields.description: Optional column defining the relative importance of each training record.

fields.name: minInfoGainGrid
fields.title: Min Info Gain Grid Search
fields.description: Candidate minimum information gain values evaluated during tuning.

fields.name: maxBinsGrid
fields.title: Max Bins Grid Search
fields.description: Different bin sizes tested to optimize split precision.

fields.name: maxDepthGrid
fields.title: Max Depth Grid Search
fields.description: Tree depth values evaluated to balance accuracy and generalization.

fields.name: numTreesGrid
fields.title: Num Trees Grid Search
fields.description: Different tree counts evaluated during hyperparameter tuning.
